Publications CV GitHub

Yuheng Zhao

Download PDF Last updated in June 2026. Get the latest version at https://zyh1222.github.io//cv.

PhD Candidate, School of Data Science, Fudan University
Research: LLM-driven interactive agents, human-AI collaborative decision-making, intelligent visual analytics
Incoming Postdoctoral Researcher, ELLIS Institute Finland

https://zyh1222.github.io/
yuhengzhao@fudan.edu.cn
+86 152-0714-7890
Google Scholar

Research Area

My research focuses on LLM-driven intelligent visual analytics, human-AI collaboration, and interactive agents for data analysis. I combine methods from Visualization, Artificial Intelligence, and Human-Computer Interaction to build intelligent interfaces that help people understand complex data, coordinate with AI agents, and make decisions in open-ended analytical workflows.

Positions

Aug 2026– ELLIS Institute Finland / Aalto University Espoo, Finland
Postdoctoral Researcher, ELLIS Institute Postdoctoral Fellowship

Supervisor: Prof. Antti Oulasvirta. Research on VLM/VLA and reinforcement-learning agents with human-in-the-loop feedback, studying when and how agents should interact with people to improve intent alignment, interaction efficiency, and autonomous decision-making in open collaborative tasks.

Jul–Sep 2023 Huawei 2012 Laboratories, Central Software Institute Shanghai, China
Research Intern

Developed LLM-agent-based data analysis methods and systems for complex scenarios such as spatiotemporal situation awareness and business intelligence, supporting task-intent understanding, analysis planning, tool use, and human-in-the-loop decision-making.

Nov 2021–Jun 2022 FDUVIS Lab, School of Data Science, Fudan University Shanghai, China
Research Assistant

Developed social media visual analytics systems, including temporal topic modeling, text comparison, and immersive 3D/VR visual analytics systems.

Jul–Sep 2020 BGI Genomics, Bioinformatics Algorithm Group Shenzhen, China
Research Intern

Designed and implemented algorithms for detecting different types of genomic structural variants from next-generation sequencing data.

Education

2022–2026 Ph.D. in Statistics, School of Data Science, Fudan University Shanghai, China

Advisor: Prof. Siming Chen. Dissertation: LLM-driven intelligent visual analytics.

2025–2026 Visiting Ph.D. Student, School of Informatics, University of Edinburgh Edinburgh, UK

Advisor: Prof. Benjamin Bach. Supported by the China Scholarship Council.

2020–2021 M.S. in Data-driven Modeling, The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology Hong Kong, China

Advisor: Prof. Michael K. Y. Wong. First Class Honors.

2016–2020 B.S. in Information and Computing Science, Huazhong Agricultural University Wuhan, China

Advisor: Prof. Feng Shi.

Selected Research Projects

2025 CellHermes: Language-based multimodal omics understanding AI for Science

Proposed a natural-language-centered framework for unifying multimodal omics data and scientific reasoning. Led the language formulation and interpretability analysis components that transform biological data into diverse instruction-response structures.

2025 ProactiveVA: Proactive visual analytics with LLM-based UI agents Human-AI Interaction

Designed a proactive GUI-agent framework that perceives user difficulties, reasons about assistance strategies, and acts within visual analytics interfaces to support data analysis.

2024 LightVA: LLM-agent-based task planning and visual analytics generation Visual Analytics

Built an LLM-agent workflow for decomposing analysis goals, generating visual analytics systems, and optimizing multiple linked views, reducing development from weeks to hours.

2023 LEVA: LLM-enhanced visual analytics Visual Analytics

Proposed an early LLM-enhanced visual analytics framework that supports onboarding, exploration, recommendation, and report generation. The paper is an ESI Highly Cited Paper (top 1%).

2023 ContextWing: Visual comparison for evolving social media contexts Social Computing

Developed a text comparison and visual analytics method that combines dynamic topic modeling and sequential pattern mining to reveal semantic evolution in large-scale social media streams.

Teaching

Fall 2021 Data Visualization and Application Fudan University

Teaching Assistant. Developed and presented tutorials on visual analytics tools, HTML, and D3.js; graded assignments for approximately 80 students.
